data.title: Worthington Steel Secures Majority Stake in Klöckner & Co
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Worthington Steel, Inc. ( (WS) ) is now available.

On March 31, 2026, Worthington Steel reported that its voluntary public cash takeover offer for German steel and metal processor Klöckner & Co SE cleared the 57.5% minimum acceptance threshold, securing about 58.8% of Klöckner’s share capital by the end of the initial acceptance period on March 26. Klöckner’s management and supervisory boards have deemed the €11-per-share all-cash offer, launched in January at a 98% premium to the prior three-month average price, attractive and recommended that shareholders tender, while an additional acceptance period runs from April 1 to April 14, 2026.

Worthington Steel plans, after expected closing in the second half of 2026 and subject to regulatory approvals, to pursue a domination and profit and loss transfer agreement with Klöckner and may consider structural steps such as a potential delisting or squeeze-out of minority shareholders. The transaction would unite one of North America’s most trusted metals processors with one of Europe’s leading producer-independent steel and metal service center groups, potentially expanding Worthington Steel’s geographic reach, service portfolio and higher value-added processing capabilities in both North America and Europe.

The most recent analyst rating on (WS) stock is a Buy with a $38.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Worthington Steel, Inc. stock, see the WS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on WS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WS is a Neutral.

The score is held back most by weak technicals (price below major moving averages, negative MACD, low RSI) and an expensive earnings multiple for a cyclical business. Fundamentals are workable but uneven—positive profitability and cash flow are offset by cyclical margin pressure and balance-sheet data consistency concerns. The earnings call adds some support via strategic and operational progress, but near-term volatility, costs, and working-capital pressure temper the outlook.

To see Spark’s full report on WS stock, click here.

More about Worthington Steel, Inc.

Worthington Steel, Inc. is a North American metals processor specializing in carbon flat-roll steel processing, electrical steel laminations and tailor welded solutions, with value-added capabilities such as galvanizing, pickling and specialty cold reduction. Headquartered in Columbus, Ohio, the company operates 37 facilities across seven U.S. states and 10 countries, employing about 6,000 people and focusing on technical, customized and sustainability-oriented steel solutions.

Average Trading Volume: 312,629

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $1.46B

See more insights into WS stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Canton Strategic Highlights 2025 Results, Advances Digital Asset Focus
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Canton Strategic Holdings ( (CNTN) ) has shared an update.

On March 31, 2026, Canton Strategic Holdings reported full-year 2025 results that underscored its transition into a Canton-centric digital asset treasury company while still operating clinical-stage biotech R&D. The company crystallized this pivot through a $545 million private placement in November 2025, large CC holdings, and approval as a Super Validator on the Canton Network, which processes more than one million daily transactions and $9 trillion in monthly onchain volume.

For 2025, Canton held about 3.34 billion CC valued at roughly $502 million and $17 million in cash, but continued to generate no revenue and recorded a net loss of $35.9 million, driven by higher operating expenses tied to the strategy shift and a $22 million unrealized loss on digital assets. Capital markets activity remained robust into early 2026, including a $55 million equity and warrant offering, leadership and board upgrades with deep trading and regulatory expertise, and a new role on the Canton Foundation Board, collectively strengthening governance and capital resources as the company seeks to monetize CC holdings and support broader institutional blockchain adoption.

Management highlighted plans for 2026 to pursue ecosystem investments on the Canton Network, explore yield-enhancing mechanisms for its CC reserves, and streamline costs after one-off compensation and administrative charges, aiming to balance growth with discipline. These moves signal an attempt to solidify Canton Strategic Holdings’ position as a specialist vehicle for institutional-grade digital asset exposure, while its lack of revenue and accumulated deficit emphasize ongoing execution, market, and valuation risks for shareholders and other stakeholders.

More about Canton Strategic Holdings

Canton Strategic Holdings, Inc., listed on NASDAQ as CNTN, is the first publicly traded company focused on leveraging Canton Coin to support the Canton Network’s institutional blockchain infrastructure and digitization of financial markets. Alongside its Canton-centric digital asset treasury and Super Validator operations, the company also maintains clinical-stage biotech research and development activities, positioning it at the intersection of digital assets and life sciences innovation.

The firm’s strategy centers on accumulating and mobilizing CC, securing and governing the Canton Network, and investing in applications that promote institutional adoption, especially among major financial institutions pursuing onchain financing, settlement, clearing, and tokenization use cases. This dual-focus model reflects an early-stage, development-oriented profile, with operations funded primarily through equity issuance and recent large capital raises to build its digital asset balance sheet and capabilities.

Average Trading Volume: 878,004

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: $158.7M

See more data about CNTN stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

Willis Lease Finance ( (WLFC) ) has issued an update.

On March 26, 2026, Willis Warehouse Facility LLC, a wholly owned subsidiary of Willis Lease Finance Corporation, entered into an agreement to terminate a credit agreement originally dated May 3, 2024, with Bank of America as facility agent, Bank of Utah as administrative agent and security trustee, and a syndicate of lenders. The move marks a change in the company’s financing arrangements, with the related termination documentation to be filed in Willis Lease Finance’s quarterly report for the period ended March 31, 2026, and comes against a backdrop of ongoing operational and economic uncertainties affecting the aviation industry and the company’s cost structure and customer demand.

To see Spark’s full report on WLFC stock, click here.

Willis Lease Finance Corporation operates in the aviation finance sector, primarily through subsidiaries such as Willis Warehouse Facility LLC. The company’s business is closely tied to general economic conditions and demand in the aviation industry, and it is exposed to risks including labor disputes, supply chain constraints, foreign currency fluctuations and broader macroeconomic and trade policy uncertainties.

data.title: Ellington Residential Mortgage Completes $50 Million Notes Offering
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ellington Residential Mortgage ( (EARN) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 30, 2026, Ellington Credit Company closed a previously announced public offering of $50 million of 8.50% Notes due 2031, with underwriters granted a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional $7.5 million. The notes, expected to list on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol “ELLA” within 30 days of issuance, are general unsecured obligations that rank senior to future expressly subordinated debt, pari passu with existing senior indebtedness and general liabilities, and structurally or effectively junior to secured and subsidiary-level obligations.

The notes carry a fixed 8.50% annual interest rate, payable quarterly starting June 30, 2026, and are issued in $25 denominations with no sinking fund or holder put option before their March 30, 2031 maturity. Ellington Credit may redeem the notes at par plus accrued interest on or after March 30, 2028, may issue additional notes of the same series without holder consent, and is bound by covenants tied to 1940 Act asset coverage and ongoing financial disclosure, shaping the capital structure and protections available to noteholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (EARN) stock is a Buy with a $5.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ellington Residential Mortgage stock, see the EARN Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on EARN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, EARN is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by inconsistent profitability and weak/negative free cash flow, despite balance-sheet improvement (zero debt) and constructive technical momentum. Valuation is supported by a moderate P/E and very high dividend yield, with recent corporate updates reinforcing dividend commitment.

To see Spark’s full report on EARN stock, click here.

More about Ellington Residential Mortgage

Ellington Credit Company is a closed-end fund that issues debt securities to finance its investment activities in credit-related assets. The fund operates in the credit and fixed-income markets, using public note offerings to raise capital while remaining subject to investment company regulations, including the asset coverage requirements of the Investment Company Act of 1940.

Eos Energy Enterprises ( (EOSE) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 30, 2026, Eos Energy Enterprises, Inc. and its subsidiaries entered into a new employment agreement with Chief Administration Officer Michelle Buczkowski, replacing her prior offer letter and setting her annual base salary at $385,000, with eligibility for a year-end target bonus equal to 75% of base pay and annual long-term incentive grants. The agreement outlines severance protections if she is terminated without cause or resigns for good reason, including 12 months of continued base salary, certain bonus payments, partial vesting of equity awards, and imposes ongoing confidentiality, intellectual property, non-compete, and non-solicitation obligations for 12 months post-employment, underscoring the company’s effort to retain senior leadership and protect strategic interests.

data.title: Prologis Expands Global Credit Facility to Enhance Liquidity
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Prologis ( (PLD) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On March 26, 2026, Prologis, L.P. and affiliated borrowers entered into an Amended and Restated Global Senior Credit Agreement providing a revolving, multi-currency credit facility of approximately $3 billion, with an accordion feature of up to an additional $1 billion, split between a $2 billion U.S. Dollar Tranche and a €864.2 million Euro Tranche. The facility, maturing June 28, 2030 with options to extend to as late as June 30, 2031, is priced at a spread that was 65 basis points at closing, includes customary financial covenants and cross-acceleration defaults, and is fully guaranteed by the Operating Partnership, while leaving Prologis, Inc. unrequired to guarantee unless it takes on new indebtedness, and a concurrent amendment aligned key terms of a separate 2025 global credit facility with the new 2026 structure.

These changes bolster Prologis’ liquidity, extend its debt maturity profile, and harmonize documentation across major bank facilities, likely enhancing financial flexibility and reducing refinancing risk for stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (PLD) stock is a Buy with a $153.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Prologis stock, see the PLD Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on PLD Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PLD is a Outperform.

The score reflects strong technical momentum and a positive earnings outlook (high occupancy, embedded NOI, and active development pipeline), balanced against mixed 2025 financial quality signals (margin compression, higher leverage, and weaker cash flow coverage) and a relatively expensive valuation (high P/E).

To see Spark’s full report on PLD stock, click here.

More about Prologis

Prologis, L.P. operates as part of Prologis, Inc., a global leader in logistics real estate that finances its operations through large-scale credit facilities and capital markets access. The partnership structure allows Prologis to tap multi-currency bank lending, supporting its portfolio of distribution and logistics properties across key international markets.

data.title: TEAM plc transforms into international wealth platform with WH Ireland and EPIC deals
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

TEAM plc ( (GB:TEAM) ) has provided an update.

TEAM plc, the wealth, asset management and complementary financial services group, reported strong organic growth for the year to 30 September 2025, with revenues up 16% to £12.0m and client assets rising 11% to £1.29bn. All three divisions contributed, led by its International arm, while Investment Management expanded via model portfolio services and new UCITS funds and Advisory grew as more clients moved into discretionary mandates, helping narrow adjusted EBITDA losses as operational leverage improved.

Since the year end, TEAM has executed a step-change in scale by completing the all-share acquisition of UK wealth manager WH Ireland and announcing two EPIC transactions, collectively adding around £1bn of assets under management. These deals establish a meaningful FCA-regulated UK presence, lift group AUM to more than £2.3bn, broaden the client base and infrastructure, and are intended to accelerate growth by shifting the mix toward higher-quality recurring revenues and tighter integration of advice and investment management across UK and international markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:TEAM) stock is a Hold with a £26.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on TEAM plc stock, see the GB:TEAM Stock Forecast page.

TEAM plc’s overall stock score is primarily impacted by its financial performance and technical analysis. The company’s strong revenue growth is overshadowed by profitability and cash flow challenges. Technical indicators suggest bearish momentum, and valuation metrics are unfavorable due to ongoing losses.

To see Spark’s full report on TEAM stock, click here.

TEAM plc is a wealth and asset management group providing discretionary investment management, model and bespoke portfolios, fund management, advisory and international financial services. The company focuses on International Finance Centres and key regions including Jersey, Guernsey, the UAE, Malaysia, Singapore and South Africa, serving wealthy individuals, trusts, institutions and international clients through its Investment Management, Advisory and International divisions.

DCC plc ( (GB:DCC) ) has issued an update.

DCC plc has reported that, as of 31 March 2026, it has 87,609,229 issued ordinary shares of €0.25 each, of which 2,185,094 are held in treasury and do not carry voting rights. This leaves 85,424,135 ordinary shares in issue with voting rights, a key reference figure for investors monitoring their holdings under U.K. disclosure and transparency rules.

The updated share capital and voting rights structure is primarily relevant for regulatory reporting and compliance purposes under the FCA’s Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules. Shareholders must now use the 85,424,135 voting shares as the denominator when assessing whether changes in their positions trigger notification obligations, ensuring continued clarity over ownership thresholds and market transparency.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:DCC) stock is a Buy with a £4984.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on DCC plc stock, see the GB:DCC Stock Forecast page.
